selected_important_turn.text: Would you be willing to let me have all the firewood and you could take 2 of the food and 2 of the water?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the key turning point where you move from cooperative framing into a demanding, zero-sum request right after the opponent expressed willingness to cooperate. It escalates tension by asking the opponent to surrender all of the speaker's top item and could have undone the cooperative dynamic.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The opponent had just signaled cooperation; instead of capitalizing on that to build trust and search for integrative trades, you made a high-demand, self-favoring offer that disregards the opponent's revealed priorities. It's mistimed and risks appearing greedy, increasing the chance of refusal and conflict.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift away from an all-or-nothing demand and frame a trade or question: acknowledge their cooperation, ask whether they have any constraints about giving up some firewood, or propose a conditional trade (e.g., 'Would you accept me taking more firewood if I give up X?'). Offer a small concession or invite the opponent to propose alternatives so you keep the cooperative frame.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — opening with a conditional trade or asking questions can reveal integrative options and create value; the current demand narrows possibilities.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — a negotiated trade or conditional offer is perceived as more balanced than a unilateral take-all ask.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a cooperative question or trade will make it easier to reach agreement than a hard demand that invites refusal.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — preserving a collaborative tone and asking for input builds trust; the current phrasing risks harming rapport.

value2reason.Low: Having more water would help me have enough to wash and clean up.
value2reason.Medium: I need some more food in case I decide to stay an extra day.
value2reason.High: I need more firewood because I forgot to bring some from home.

value2reason.Low: Because if I didn't have a fire I wouldn't really need food or water cause I couldn't cook
value2reason.Medium: Because food is the only thing I need something to accompany it.
value2reason.High: Additional packages along with water is helpful because I can use the water to supplment other things

dialogue.text: I need some firewood because I did not bring enough, I was thinking I could take 2 firewood, 1 food, and 2 water. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I can do that for you!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Ok, so you must prefer water and food over firewood. We might have lucked out as far as our priorities go!🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes pretty much that sounds like we can work something out
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Would you be willing to let me have all the firewood and you could take 2 of the food and 2 of the water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Sure, I can take 2 food and 2 water, and you can have all the firewood. It seems fair given our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Thanks for the offer! I really appreciate the cooperation. Would you be open to giving me all 3 firewood if I give you 1 food and 1 water instead? It feels like a fair trade given our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds like a good deal. Let me check the numbers again. If I take 2 food and 2 water, and you take all the firewood, we split all 9 packages. Let me confirm that we’re both okay with this.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
